response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems developer with a broad technical background, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The single most disqualifying factor is the hard requirement for native-level Japanese proficiency, which he does not possess — his listed languages are English, Romanian, and German. Beyond that, his career has trended strongly toward web, TypeScript/Node.js, and cloud-native work; the role demands deep embedded C programming, Linux kernel modification, and domain expertise in network security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, switch chip SDKs) that are entirely absent from his resume.
strengths: Long career with genuine breadth across embedded, systems, and web contexts, including C/C++ work at National Instruments and an audio synthesis thesis in C++
Demonstrated low-level and performance-sensitive development experience (DLang async I/O at Sociomantic, hardware work at Altom) suggests capacity for systems-level thinking
Strong engineering hygiene — TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and public talks indicate a disciplined and collaborative developer
concerns: Does not meet the native-level Japanese language requirement, which is a non-negotiable listed qualification
No evidence of networking domain knowledge: L2/L3 switching, MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, SNMP, or any LAN switch product experience
Current and recent trajectory is entirely web and TypeScript/cloud; C programming and Linux kernel work are limited to early or peripheral roles over a decade ago

key_factors.factor: No networking or embedded security domain experience
